skill.name: claude-speed-reader
skill.description: -Speed read Claude's responses at 600+ WPM using RSVP with Spritz-style ORP highlighting
skill.summary: -Speed read Claude's responses at 600+ WPM using RSVP with Spritz-style ORP highlighting
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Security scan detected 4 patterns (2 URLs, 2 crypto) but all are false positives. The URLs are legitimate GitHub references in documentation. The 'cryptographic algorithm' detections are misidentifications of 'RSVP' (Rapid Serial Visual Presentation) - a reading technique, not cryptography. This is a simple documentation skill with no executable code or security risks.

high_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Detection (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected hardcoded URLs at lines 4 and 22. These are legitimate GitHub source links in the documentation frontmatter and footer. This is standard documentation practice, not a security risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documented source references, not network exfiltration or malicious connections

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Static scanner misidentified 'RSVP' (Rapid Serial Visual Presentation) as a weak cryptographic algorithm. This is a reading speed technique, not cryptography. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: RSVP stands for Rapid Serial Visual Presentation - a well-known speed reading method. No encryption or crypto algorithms present.

content.user_title: Speed Read Claude Responses at 600+ WPM
content.value_statement: Claude provides detailed, thorough responses that can be time-consuming to read. This skill enables rapid serial visual presentation (RSVP) with ORP highlighting to read at 600+ words per minute.
actual_capabilities: Enable RSVP mode for displaying Claude responses word-by-word at high speed
Apply Spritz-style ORP (Optimal Recognition Point) highlighting to improve reading speed
Configure reading speed from 300 to 1000+ WPM
Pause and resume reading at any point
Navigate back to previous words or sections
limitations: Requires manual activation for each response
Not suitable for code or technical content that requires careful review
May lose context when skipping detailed explanations
Effectiveness varies by individual reading ability

use_cases.title: Quick Overview of Long Responses
use_cases.description: Get the gist of verbose Claude responses quickly when you need the key points fast
use_cases.target_user: Power users who receive frequent detailed responses

use_cases.title: Scanning Multiple Responses
use_cases.description: Rapidly scan through conversation history to find specific information
use_cases.target_user: Researchers and developers reviewing past conversations

use_cases.title: Improving Reading Speed
use_cases.description: Practice and improve RSVP reading skills with Claude's help
use_cases.target_user: Anyone looking to increase their reading speed

prompt_templates.title: Activate Speed Reader
prompt_templates.prompt: Activate the speed reader mode for your next response. Use RSVP with ORP highlighting at 600 WPM.

prompt_templates.title: Adjust Reading Speed
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the last response at [WPM] words per minute using RSVP mode.

prompt_templates.title: Resume from Checkpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Resume speed reading from where I stopped. Continue at the last checkpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Scan Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Give me a quick 1000 WPM scan of this response to get the main points, then slow down on the key sections.

output_examples.output: Speed reader activated. Your response will appear using RSVP with ORP highlighting at 600 WPM. Press SPACE to pause, arrow keys to navigate. Click anywhere to begin.
Resuming from checkpoint 3 of your previous response. Continue at 600 WPM or adjust speed with +/- keys.

best_practices: Start with lower speeds (200-300 WPM) and gradually increase as you get comfortable
Use pause frequently to review complex concepts before continuing
Set checkpoints at important sections to easily navigate back
anti_patterns: Using speed reading for code review or technical content that requires careful analysis
Setting speed too high too quickly, resulting in poor comprehension
Skipping over error messages or warnings in responses

faq.question: What is RSVP reading?
faq.answer: RSVP (Rapid Serial Visual Presentation) shows words one at a time in a fixed position, eliminating eye movement and allowing faster reading.

faq.question: What is ORP highlighting?
faq.answer: ORP (Optimal Recognition Point) highlights the pivot letter in each word where the eye naturally focuses, improving recognition speed.

faq.question: How fast can I read with this skill?
faq.answer: Most users start at 300-400 WPM and can progress to 600-800 WPM with practice. Elite users achieve 1000+ WPM.

faq.question: Can I use this for code?
faq.answer: This is not recommended for code. Code requires careful review of syntax and structure that RSVP cannot provide.

faq.question: Does this work with all Claude responses?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can be applied to any text response. Some content like tables and code blocks may not display optimally.

faq.question: How do I pause and navigate?
faq.answer: Use SPACE to pause, arrow keys to move forward/backward, and number keys to jump to checkpoints.
